notes: All 21 steps completed (1 prerequisite + 20 TDD steps). Implemented autopilot/render/validate.py with validate_render(), ValidationReport, Issue, ValidationError. Six quality checks: duration, resolution/codec, file size, loudness, black frames, silence detection. All 45 tests pass. Updated render/__init__.py exports.

summary: Create empty autopilot/render/validate.py with module docstring and __all__; Write failing tests for public API surface: ValidationReport dataclass (passed, issues list with severity/message/measurement, measurements dict), ValidationError exception, validate_render function signature (rendered_path: Path, edl: dict, config: OutputConfig) -> ValidationReport, and __all__ exports; Implement ValidationReport dataclass with Issue(severity, check, message, measured_value) inner dataclass, ValidationError exception class, validate_render stub returning empty ValidationReport, and __all__ exports. Update render/__init__.py to export new symbols.; Write failing tests for _run_ffprobe_json helper: mock subprocess.run to return ffprobe JSON with streams and format info; verify it extracts duration, resolution, codec, file size; test error handling when ffprobe fails (returns empty dict or raises); Implement _run_ffprobe_json(path) that calls ffprobe -v quiet -print_format json -show_format -show_streams, parses stdout JSON, returns dict with duration_seconds, resolution (w,h), video_codec, audio_codec, file_size_bytes. Handle subprocess errors gracefully.; Write failing tests for duration check: mock ffprobe to return duration. Test pass when within ±1s of EDL target_duration_seconds; test error when duration off by >1s; test warning/skip when edl has no target_duration_seconds; Implement _check_duration(probe_data, edl, issues) comparing probe duration to edl['target_duration_seconds'] within ±1s tolerance, appending Issue with severity='error' when out of range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for resolution and codec check: test pass when probe resolution matches config.resolution and codec matches config.codec; test error on resolution mismatch; test error on codec mismatch; Implement _check_resolution_codec(probe_data, config, issues) comparing probe resolution/codec against OutputConfig values, appending error Issues on mismatch. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for file size check: compute expected range as 8-15 MB per minute for 1080p h264. Test pass when file size within range; test warning when below 8 MB/min; test warning when above 15 MB/min; test that non-1080p or non-h264 skips the check; Implement _check_file_size(probe_data, config, issues) computing per-minute bitrate and comparing to 8-15 MB/min threshold for 1080p h264, appending warning Issues when outside range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for loudness check: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg loudnorm analysis pass (ffmpeg -i file -af loudnorm=print_format=json -f null -). Parse measured LUFS from stderr JSON. Test pass when within -16±1 LUFS; test error when outside range; test error when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_loudness(rendered_path, config, issues) running ffmpeg loudnorm analysis pass, parsing measured integrated loudness from stderr JSON output, appending error Issue when outside target_loudness_lufs ± 1 LUFS. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for black frame detection: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg blackdetect filter. Test pass when no black frames detected; test warning when black frames found (stderr contains blackdetect output); test graceful handling when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_black_frames(rendered_path, issues) running ffmpeg -i file -vf blackdetect=d=0.1:pix_th=5 -f null -, parsing blackdetect output from stderr for black_start/black_end/black_duration fields, appending warning Issues for each detected range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for silence detection: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg silencedetect filter. Test pass when no silence >2s detected; test warning when silence >2s found; test that intentional silence marked in EDL is excluded; test graceful handling when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_silence(rendered_path, edl, issues) running ffmpeg -i file -af silencedetect=n=-50dB:d=2 -f null -, parsing silence_start/silence_end/silence_duration from stderr, filtering out EDL-marked intentional silences, appending warning Issues. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for validate_render end-to-end: mock all subprocess calls. Test full pass scenario returns passed=True with measurements dict populated. Test mixed issues scenario returns passed=False with correct error/warning counts. Test report JSON serialization.; Complete validate_render orchestration: call ffprobe once, run all checks, populate measurements dict (duration, loudness_lufs, file_size_mb, resolution, codec), set passed=True only if no error-severity issues, return ValidationReport. Add to_dict() method for JSON serialization.; Write failing tests for validation_report.json writing: validate_render should write validation_report.json to output/{narrative_title}/ directory. Test file is created with correct content. Test handles missing output directory by creating it.; Add report writing to validate_render: derive output directory from rendered_path parent, write validation_report.json with JSON-serialized ValidationReport. Create directory if needed.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues
Completed all 6 remaining robustness steps (21-26). Added ValueError guards for _check_black_frames and _check_silence float() parsing, and OSError guard for report file writing. All 51 tests pass.

Completed all 20 steps + 1 prerequisite for YouTube upload and thumbnail task. Created autopilot/upload/youtube.py (UploadError, upload_video with OAuth creds, metadata assembly, resumable upload, retry), autopilot/upload/thumbnail.py (ThumbnailError, extract_best_thumbnail with Laplacian sharpness, rule-of-thirds, detection confidence scoring, frame extraction, optional YouTube thumbnail upload), scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py (OAuth setup script). Updated upload/__init__.py exports. All 35 tests pass.

Update autopilot/upload/__init__.py to export public symbols from youtube and thumbnail modules (UploadError, upload_video, ThumbnailError, extract_best_thumbnail); Write failing tests for youtube.py public API surface: UploadError importable and is Exception, upload_video signature has (narrative_id, video_path, db, config) params, __all__ exports UploadError and upload_video; Create autopilot/upload/youtube.py with UploadError exception class, upload_video stub function with correct signature (narrative_id: str, video_path: Path, db: CatalogDB, config: YouTubeConfig) -> str, and __all__ exports. Function raises NotImplementedError for now.; Write failing tests for OAuth credential loading: test_loads_credentials_from_config_path (mocks google.oauth2.credentials.Credentials.from_authorized_user_file), test_raises_upload_error_when_credentials_file_missing (no creds file -> UploadError), test_refreshes_expired_credentials (mocks creds.expired=True + creds.refresh_token set -> calls creds.refresh with google.auth.transport.requests.Request); Implement _load_credentials(credentials_path: Path) helper that loads OAuth2 credentials from file via Credentials.from_authorized_user_file, checks creds.expired and creds.refresh_token, refreshes via creds.refresh(Request()) if needed, and raises UploadError on missing file or refresh failure; Write failing tests for metadata assembly: test_builds_title_from_narrative (title from db.get_narrative), test_builds_description_from_script (description from db.get_narrative_script), test_tags_from_activity_labels_and_detections (extracts unique object class names from detections JSON + activity cluster labels), test_uses_config_privacy_status_and_category (privacyStatus and categoryId from YouTubeConfig); Implement _build_upload_metadata(narrative_id, db, config) that queries CatalogDB for narrative title/description, narrative_script for full description, activity_clusters for labels (tags), and detections for unique object class names (tags). Returns dict with 'snippet' (title, description, tags, categoryId) and 'status' (privacyStatus) suitable for YouTube API body.; Write failing tests for the full upload_video flow: test_upload_calls_youtube_api_insert (mocks googleapiclient.discovery.build, verifies videos().insert() called with MediaFileUpload), test_upload_stores_result_in_db (catalog_db fixture: verifies db.insert_upload called with youtube_video_id and youtube_url after upload), test_upload_returns_youtube_url (returns 'https://youtu.be/<video_id>'), test_upload_raises_on_api_error (HttpError from API wrapped as UploadError); Implement the full upload_video() function: load credentials via _load_credentials, build metadata via _build_upload_metadata, create YouTube service via googleapiclient.discovery.build('youtube', 'v3', credentials=creds), call videos().insert() with MediaFileUpload(video_path, resumable=True), execute resumable upload via next_chunk() loop, store result in DB via db.insert_upload, return YouTube URL; Write failing tests for upload_video edge cases: test_narrative_not_found_raises_upload_error (narrative_id not in DB -> UploadError), test_video_file_not_found_raises_upload_error (video_path doesn't exist -> UploadError), test_resumable_upload_retry (simulates HttpError on first next_chunk, success on retry); Add input validation and retry logic to upload_video: validate narrative exists in DB (raise UploadError if not), validate video_path.exists() (raise UploadError if not), implement resumable upload retry loop with exponential backoff for transient HttpError (max 3 retries); Write failing tests for thumbnail.py public API surface: ThumbnailError importable and is Exception, extract_best_thumbnail signature has (narrative_id, video_path, db) params, __all__ exports ThumbnailError and extract_best_thumbnail; Create autopilot/upload/thumbnail.py with ThumbnailError exception class, extract_best_thumbnail stub with correct signature (narrative_id: str, video_path: Path, db: CatalogDB) -> Path, and __all__ exports. Function raises NotImplementedError for now.; Write failing tests for frame scoring functions: test_sharpness_score_uses_laplacian_variance (mock cv2.Laplacian, verify variance computed on result), test_rule_of_thirds_score_high_at_intersections (numpy frame with subject bbox at thirds intersection scores higher than bbox at dead center), test_detection_confidence_score_aggregates (max confidence from list of detections dicts), test_combined_score_weighted (verify the 3 component scores are combined with weights summing to 1.0); Implement scoring functions: _sharpness_score(frame: ndarray) -> float using cv2.Laplacian variance, _rule_of_thirds_score(frame_shape, detections: list[dict]) -> float using bbox center distances to 4 thirds-grid intersection points, _detection_confidence_score(detections: list[dict]) -> float using max confidence across all detections, _combined_score(sharpness, thirds, confidence) -> float with configurable weights (default: 0.3 sharpness, 0.3 thirds, 0.4 confidence); Write failing tests for frame extraction: test_extracts_best_frame_as_jpeg (mocks cv2.VideoCapture to return frames, verifies highest-scoring frame is saved as JPEG via cv2.imwrite), test_samples_frames_at_intervals (verifies frames are sampled every N frames rather than reading every frame by checking cap.set calls), test_returns_thumbnail_path (returns Path to the saved JPEG in video_path.parent); Implement _extract_candidate_frames() that opens video with cv2.VideoCapture, gets total frame count and fps, samples frames at ~1fps intervals using cap.set(CAP_PROP_POS_FRAMES), scores each with combined scoring, and saves the best frame as JPEG via cv2.imwrite. Wire into extract_best_thumbnail flow.; Write failing tests for thumbnail upload to YouTube: test_uploads_thumbnail_via_api (mocks YouTube service thumbnails().set() API call with youtube_video_id from uploads table), test_thumbnail_upload_error_logged_not_raised (API error logged as warning, does not raise), test_no_youtube_upload_skips_thumbnail_upload (when no upload record in DB, still returns thumbnail path without calling thumbnails API); Implement YouTube thumbnail upload in extract_best_thumbnail: check uploads table via db.get_upload(narrative_id) for youtube_video_id, if present load credentials and call thumbnails().set(videoId=youtube_video_id, media_body=MediaFileUpload(thumb_path, mimetype='image/jpeg')), handle API errors gracefully (log warning, don't raise). Return thumbnail path unconditionally.; Write failing test for scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py: test_oauth_flow_saves_credentials (mocks google_auth_oauthlib.flow.InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file and run_local_server, verifies credentials serialized and written to output path), test_main_requires_client_secrets_arg (verifies argparse requires --client-secrets); Create scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py: argparse with --client-secrets (required) and --output (default ~/.config/autopilot/youtube_oauth.json). Uses InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file with YOUTUBE_UPLOAD_SCOPE, calls flow.run_local_server(port=0), serializes credentials to JSON (creds.to_json()), writes to output path. Includes if __name__ == '__main__' block.
Debug fix for: Failures: type errors

Completed all 6 review fix steps (21-26). Step 21/22: Added try/finally guard around VideoCapture scoring loop to prevent file descriptor leaks on exceptions. Step 23/24: Added cv2.imwrite return value check, returning None on failure instead of path to non-existent file. Step 25/26: Added break after successful upload in retry for-loop to exit early. All 38 tests pass.

Completed all 27 steps (1 prerequisite + 26 TDD steps). Replaced all 9 stub stage functions with real implementations wiring to downstream modules: _run_ingest (scanner, normalizer, dedup), _run_analyze (asr, scenes, objects, faces, embeddings, audio_events, GPUScheduler), _run_classify (cluster, classify), _run_narrate (narratives, human_review_fn callback), _run_script (per-narrative error isolation), _run_edl (generate, validate, export_otio), _run_source_assets (resolve), _run_render (route_and_render, validate_render), _run_upload (youtube, thumbnail). Enhanced progress reporting with cumulative budget tracking and summary table. Added human_review_fn parameter to PipelineOrchestrator. Updated CLI run subcommand with interactive review prompt. Created comprehensive e2e test. All 91 tests pass (59 orchestrator + 31 CLI + 1 e2e).

Add e2e test fixtures to conftest.py: a minimal_config fixture that creates an AutopilotConfig with tmp_path-based directories, and a mock_gpu_scheduler fixture that provides a no-op GPUScheduler-like context manager.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestIngestStage class with test_ingest_calls_scan_directory (mock scanner.scan_directory returns list of MediaFile, verify called with config.input_dir), test_ingest_inserts_media_into_db (verify db.insert_media called for each scanned file), test_ingest_normalizes_audio (mock normalizer.normalize_audio called for each media file), test_ingest_marks_duplicates (verify dedup.mark_duplicates called with db).; Implement _run_ingest(config, db) in orchestrator.py: imports scanner, normalizer, dedup. Calls scan_directory(config.input_dir), inserts each MediaFile into db via db.insert_media(), calls normalize_audio for each media file with audio (output_dir=config.output_dir/'normalized', root_dir=config.input_dir), then calls mark_duplicates(db). Replace the INGEST stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestAnalyzeStage class with test_analyze_creates_gpu_scheduler (mock GPUScheduler, verify instantiated with config.processing params), test_analyze_runs_all_analysis_per_media (mock all 6 analysis functions, verify each called for each media in DB), test_analyze_calls_cluster_faces_after_analysis (verify cluster_faces called once after all per-media analysis), test_analyze_runs_audio_and_video_concurrently (verify ThreadPoolExecutor used for parallel audio/video analysis).; Implement _run_analyze(config, db) in orchestrator.py: creates GPUScheduler, lists all media from DB with status!='duplicate', for each media file runs video analysis (scenes, objects, faces, embeddings) and audio analysis (asr, audio_events) using concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for parallelism, then calls cluster_faces(db). Replace the ANALYZE stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestClassifyStage class with test_classify_calls_cluster_activities (mock cluster.cluster_activities, verify called with db), test_classify_calls_label_activities (mock classify.label_activities, verify called with db and config.llm).; Implement _run_classify(config, db) in orchestrator.py: calls cluster_activities(db), then label_activities(db, config.llm). Replace the CLASSIFY stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestNarrateStage class with test_narrate_builds_storyboard (mock build_master_storyboard, verify called with db), test_narrate_proposes_narratives (mock propose_narratives, verify called with storyboard, db, config), test_narrate_calls_human_review_callback (verify human_review_fn is called with formatted text and list of Narrative objects), test_narrate_auto_approves_when_no_callback (without human_review_fn all narratives get status='approved'), test_narrate_respects_review_rejections (human_review_fn returns subset of IDs, rejected ones get status='rejected').; Implement _run_narrate(config, db, human_review_fn=None) in orchestrator.py: calls build_master_storyboard(db), propose_narratives(storyboard, db, config), format_for_review(narratives), invokes human_review_fn(formatted, narratives) if provided (returns approved narrative_ids), defaults to all-approved if no callback. Updates statuses in DB. Replace the NARRATE stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestScriptStage class with test_script_generates_per_approved_narrative (mock generate_script, seed DB with 2 approved narratives, verify called once per narrative), test_script_continues_on_per_narrative_error (first narrative raises ScriptError, second still gets generated), test_script_raises_if_all_narratives_fail (if every narrative fails, stage itself raises).; Implement _run_script(config, db) in orchestrator.py: lists narratives with status='approved', calls generate_script(narrative_id, db, config.llm) for each with try/except per narrative. Logs errors but continues. Raises RuntimeError only if ALL narratives failed. Replace the SCRIPT stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestEdlStage class with test_edl_generates_validates_exports_per_narrative (mock generate_edl, validate_edl, export_otio, verify all 3 called for each narrative with a script), test_edl_stores_validation_result (verify db.upsert_edit_plan stores validation_json), test_edl_continues_on_failure (one narrative fails, others still processed).; Implement _run_edl(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each approved narrative with a script, calls generate_edl(narrative_id, db, config.llm), validate_edl(edl, db), export_otio(edl, config.output_dir/narrative_id/timeline.otio, db). Stores results via db.upsert_edit_plan. Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the EDL stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestSourceStage class with test_source_resolves_assets_per_narrative (mock resolve_edl_assets, verify called for each narrative with an edit plan, passing correct args: edl dict, config.models, output_dir, db, narrative_id).; Implement _run_source_assets(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with an edit plan, loads EDL JSON from db.get_edit_plan, calls resolve_edl_assets(edl, config.models, config.output_dir/'assets'/narrative_id, db, narrative_id=narrative_id). Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the SOURCE_ASSETS stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestRenderStage class with test_render_routes_and_validates_per_narrative (mock route_and_render and validate_render, verify both called per narrative), test_render_logs_validation_warnings (verify validation issues are logged), test_render_continues_on_failure (one narrative's render fails, others still processed).; Implement _run_render(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with an edit plan, calls route_and_render(narrative_id, db, config.output), then validate_render(output_path, edl, config.output). Logs validation issues. Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the RENDER stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestUploadStage class with test_upload_uploads_and_thumbnails_per_narrative (mock upload_video and extract_best_thumbnail, verify both called per narrative), test_upload_continues_on_failure (one narrative fails, others still uploaded).; Implement _run_upload(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with a rendered output (check for render output path in edit_plan or a convention), calls upload_video(narrative_id, video_path, db, config.youtube) then extract_best_thumbnail(narrative_id, video_path, db). Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the UPLOAD stub.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestRealStageRegistration class with test_orchestrator_registers_real_functions_not_stubs (verify each stage's func is not a stub by checking it doesn't log 'not yet implemented'), test_orchestrator_accepts_human_review_fn_parameter (PipelineOrchestrator(human_review_fn=callback) stores it and passes to NARRATE stage).; Update PipelineOrchestrator.__init__ to register the real stage functions (_run_ingest, _run_analyze, etc.) instead of stubs. Add human_review_fn parameter that gets passed through to _run_narrate. The NARRATE stage func wraps _run_narrate with the callback via functools.partial or a lambda.; Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestEnhancedProgress class with test_run_logs_cumulative_elapsed_per_stage (after each stage, log shows cumulative time and remaining budget), test_run_logs_summary_table_at_end (final log message includes all stage names with their elapsed times).; Enhance PipelineOrchestrator.run() progress reporting: after each stage completion log cumulative elapsed vs budget (e.g., '[PROGRESS] 1200.0s / 3600.0s budget (33.3%)'), and at the end emit a summary table listing each stage with its elapsed time and status.; Write failing tests in test_cli.py: TestCLIRealStageWiring class with test_cli_run_uses_real_orchestrator (invoke 'run' with mocked stage modules, verify real stage functions are called not stubs), test_cli_ingest_calls_real_stage (invoke 'ingest', verify _run_ingest path is exercised), test_cli_individual_subcommands_call_correct_stages (each subcommand exercises the right combination of stage functions).; Update autopilot/cli.py: since PipelineOrchestrator now registers real functions, the existing CLI subcommands that call orch._stage_map[NAME].func(config=config, db=db) will automatically use the real implementations. Verify all subcommands work with the updated orchestrator. No major CLI changes needed beyond ensuring the 'run' subcommand passes human_review_fn (default to interactive input() prompt for CLI use).; Write tests/test_e2e.py: comprehensive end-to-end test using in-memory DB and mocked external dependencies. TestEndToEnd class with test_full_pipeline_smoke (mock all external calls — scanner returns fake MediaFile list, all ML functions write to DB, LLM calls return canned responses, ffmpeg mocked, YouTube API mocked — run full pipeline, verify DB state: media inserted, transcripts exist, detections stored, clusters created, narratives proposed and approved, scripts generated, EDLs stored, render output path returned, upload record created).; Fix any integration issues discovered during e2e test execution. Adjust stage function data flow, argument passing, or error handling as needed to ensure the full pipeline runs end-to-end with mocks. Ensure all existing tests in test_orchestrator.py and test_cli.py still pass.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues, type errors

Completed all 11 post-review fix steps (27-37). Five issues resolved: (1) Fixed vacuously true test assertion in test_source_resolves_assets_per_narrative. (2) Added GPUScheduler resource cleanup via try/finally with force_unload_all(). (3) Added per-media error isolation in _run_analyze with success/failure counting. (4) Added all-failure RuntimeError guard to _run_edl, _run_source_assets, _run_render, _run_upload. (5) Implemented DB-mediated RENDER→UPLOAD data contract: added render_path column to edit_plans table, _run_render persists render_path via upsert_edit_plan, _run_upload reads render_path from DB instead of filesystem convention. Also updated upsert_edit_plan to support partial updates (merge existing fields). All 103 tests pass (68 orchestrator + 34 CLI + 1 e2e).
